# 🔍 MSBuild Project Files Review — ContosoApp Solution

### 2. Test Package Version Drift — xunit ecosystem

|---------|--------------------------|---------|------------------------------|
| `ContosoApp.Core.Tests` (lines 15-17) | **17.10.0** | **2.8.1** | **2.8.1** |
| `ContosoApp.Api.Tests` (lines 15-17) | **17.9.0** | **2.7.1** | **2.5.6** |

**Fix:** Align both test projects to the same (latest) versions.

### 3. Massive Duplication — No `Directory.Build.props`

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

**Impact:** Any change (e.g., upgrading to `net9.0`) requires editing 5 files. This is the textbook case for `Directory.Build.props`.

**Fix:** Create `/Directory.Build.props`:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Then remove these 7 properties from every `.csproj`.

---

### 4. Duplicated `DocumentationFile` Setting (3 src projects)

Found in:
- `src/ContosoApp.Core/ContosoApp.Core.csproj` (line 19)
- `src/ContosoApp.Api/ContosoApp.Api.csproj` (line 25)
- `src/ContosoApp.Services/ContosoApp.Services.csproj` (line 23)

All set: `<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>`

**Fix:** Move to `Directory.Build.props` (or a `src/Directory.Build.props`) with:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```
Note: The modern SDK property `GenerateDocumentationFile` automatically sets the output path — no need to construct it manually.

---

### 5. Duplicated Custom Target `PrintBuildInfo` (3 src projects)

Found identically in:
- `src/ContosoApp.Core/ContosoApp.Core.csproj` (lines 22-24)
- `src/ContosoApp.Api/ContosoApp.Api.csproj` (lines 28-30)
- `src/ContosoApp.Services/ContosoApp.Services.csproj` (lines 26-28)

```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

**Fix:** Move to `Directory.Build.targets`:
```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

---

### 6. No Central Package Management (CPM)

There are **10 distinct packages** referenced across 5 projects with no centralized version control. This is the #1 cause of version drift (see issues #1 and #2 above).

**Fix:** Create `/Directory.Packages.props`:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Production -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <!-- Test -->

    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then remove `Version="..."` from all `<PackageReference>` elements in `.csproj` files.

---

### 7. `xunit.runner.visualstudio` Missing `PrivateAssets="all"`

Found in:
- `test/ContosoApp.Core.Tests/ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj` (line 17)
- `test/ContosoApp.Api.Tests/ContosoApp.Api.Tests.csproj` (line 17)

Test infrastructure packages (`xunit.runner.visualstudio`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) should not flow to consuming projects. While these test projects are marked `IsPackable=false` (which mitigates the risk), best practice is:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" PrivateAssets="all" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" PrivateAssets="all" />
```

---

Both test projects reference the identical set of 4 test packages. This could be consolidated into a `test/Directory.Build.props`:

```xml

    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" PrivateAssets="all" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

### 9. Split PropertyGroups Unnecessarily

In `ContosoApp.Core.csproj` (lines 3-11 and 18-20), `ContosoApp.Api.csproj` (lines 3-11 and 24-26), and `ContosoApp.Services.csproj` (lines 3-11 and 22-24), there are two separate `<PropertyGroup>` blocks that could be consolidated into one.

**Impact:** Readability. Minor, but indicates organic growth without cleanup.

### 10. Consider Replacing `Newtonsoft.Json` with `System.Text.Json`

`src/ContosoApp.Services/ContosoApp.Services.csproj` (line 15) references `Newtonsoft.Json 13.0.3`, while `ContosoApp.Core` already uses `System.Text.Json 8.0.5`. Having two JSON serializers adds dependency weight and potential confusion.

**Suggestion:** Evaluate migrating `Services` to `System.Text.Json` for consistency (unless Newtonsoft-specific features are required).

The `PrintBuildInfo` target (all 3 src projects) has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes. While this is a logging-only target that *should* run every build, it's worth noting that adding `Inputs`/`Outputs` to any future custom targets is critical for incremental build correctness.

| ✅ `TreatWarningsAsErrors` | Applied consistently |
| ✅ `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` | Applied consistently |
| ✅ `IsPackable>false` on test projects | Correctly set |
| ✅ Clean project references | No circular dependencies |
| ✅ No hardcoded paths | All paths use MSBuild properties |

| Priority | Action | Effort | Impact |
|----------|--------|--------|--------|
| 1 | Create `Directory.Build.props` at repo root | Low | High — eliminates 7 duplicated properties × 5 files |
| 2 | Create `Directory.Packages.props` (CPM) | Low | High — prevents version drift permanently |
| 3 | Create `Directory.Build.targets` for `PrintBuildInfo` | Low | Medium — eliminates duplicated target |
| 4 | Create `test/Directory.Build.props` for test packages | Low | Medium — eliminates test package duplication |
| 5 | Fix version drift (Logging.Abstractions, xunit) | Low | High — eliminates potential runtime issues |
| 6 | Replace manual `DocumentationFile` with `GenerateDocumentationFile` | Trivial | Low — modernization |
| 7 | Add `PrivateAssets="all"` to test infra packages | Trivial | Low — correctness |
| 8 | Evaluate Newtonsoft.Json → System.Text.Json migration | Medium | Medium — reduces dependencies |

After these changes, the individual `.csproj` files would shrink dramatically. For example, `ContosoApp.Core.csproj` would become:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />

  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

That's a reduction from **26 lines to 5 lines** — much easier to maintain.

**Fix:** Unify to `8.0.1` across all projects (or better yet, adopt Central Package Management — see below).

### 2. Test Package Version Drift — xunit ecosystem

| Project | `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | `xunit` | `xunit.runner.visualstudio` |
|---------|--------------------------|---------|------------------------------|
| `ContosoApp.Core.Tests` (lines 15-17) | **17.10.0** | **2.8.1** | **2.8.1** |
| `ContosoApp.Api.Tests` (lines 15-17) | **17.9.0** | **2.7.1** | **2.5.6** |

**Impact:** Different test infrastructure versions can lead to inconsistent test behavior, discovery issues, or outright failures. The `Api.Tests` project is using older, mismatched versions of the xunit runner.

**Fix:** Align both test projects to the same (latest) versions.

## 🟡 Warnings (Anti-patterns to Fix)

### 3. Massive Duplication — No `Directory.Build.props`

The following properties are **copy-pasted identically** in all 5 project files:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

**Impact:** Any change (e.g., upgrading to `net9.0`) requires editing 5 files. This is the textbook case for `Directory.Build.props`.

**Fix:** Create `/Directory.Build.props`:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Then remove these 7 properties from every `.csproj`.

---

### 4. Duplicated `DocumentationFile` Setting (3 src projects)

Found in:
- `src/ContosoApp.Core/ContosoApp.Core.csproj` (line 19)
- `src/ContosoApp.Api/ContosoApp.Api.csproj` (line 25)
- `src/ContosoApp.Services/ContosoApp.Services.csproj` (line 23)

All set: `<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>`

**Fix:** Move to `Directory.Build.props` (or a `src/Directory.Build.props`) with:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```
Note: The modern SDK property `GenerateDocumentationFile` automatically sets the output path — no need to construct it manually.

---

### 5. Duplicated Custom Target `PrintBuildInfo` (3 src projects)

Found identically in:
- `src/ContosoApp.Core/ContosoApp.Core.csproj` (lines 22-24)
- `src/ContosoApp.Api/ContosoApp.Api.csproj` (lines 28-30)
- `src/ContosoApp.Services/ContosoApp.Services.csproj` (lines 26-28)

```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

**Fix:** Move to `Directory.Build.targets`:
```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

---

### 6. No Central Package Management (CPM)

There are **10 distinct packages** referenced across 5 projects with no centralized version control. This is the #1 cause of version drift (see issues #1 and #2 above).

**Fix:** Create `/Directory.Packages.props`:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Production -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <!-- Test -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then remove `Version="..."` from all `<PackageReference>` elements in `.csproj` files.

---

### 7. `xunit.runner.visualstudio` Missing `PrivateAssets="all"`

Found in:
- `test/ContosoApp.Core.Tests/ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj` (line 17)
- `test/ContosoApp.Api.Tests/ContosoApp.Api.Tests.csproj` (line 17)

Test infrastructure packages (`xunit.runner.visualstudio`, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) should not flow to consuming projects. While these test projects are marked `IsPackable=false` (which mitigates the risk), best practice is:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" PrivateAssets="all" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" PrivateAssets="all" />
```

---

### 8. Duplicated Test Infrastructure Across Test Projects

Both test projects reference the identical set of 4 test packages. This could be consolidated into a `test/Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" PrivateAssets="all" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

## 🔵 Suggestions (Improvements)

### 9. Split PropertyGroups Unnecessarily

In `ContosoApp.Core.csproj` (lines 3-11 and 18-20), `ContosoApp.Api.csproj` (lines 3-11 and 24-26), and `ContosoApp.Services.csproj` (lines 3-11 and 22-24), there are two separate `<PropertyGroup>` blocks that could be consolidated into one.

`src/ContosoApp.Services/ContosoApp.Services.csproj` (line 15) references `Newtonsoft.Json 13.0.3`, while `ContosoApp.Core` already uses `System.Text.Json 8.0.5`. Having two JSON serializers adds dependency weight and potential confusion.

### 11. Custom Target Lacks `Inputs`/`Outputs`

The `PrintBuildInfo` target (all 3 src projects) has no `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes. While this is a logging-only target that *should* run every build, it's worth noting that adding `Inputs`/`Outputs` to any future custom targets is critical for incremental build correctness.

|----------|--------|
| ✅ SDK-style projects | All 5 projects use modern SDK-style format |

| ✅ `TreatWarningsAsErrors` | Applied consistently |

After these changes, the individual `.csproj` files would shrink dramatically. For example, `ContosoApp.Core.csproj` would become:
